heading: ExceptionDay
text: An ExceptionDay specifies a day or days that recursExcept and recursAddition use to specify unique days that do not recur on the same day each year, for example, holidays.

heading: CdmThing
text: Added for organizational purposes, to identify all classes defined in the City Data Model.

heading: PlannedAllocation
text:
Specifies the planned allocation of a resource to an activity via a state.
        Note that Allocation is a Manifestation as the allocation may change over time.

heading: FoundationalCdmThing
text: Added for organizational purposes, to identify all classes defined in the Foundation-level CDM ontology.

heading: ConjunctiveAgreement
text: A conjunctive agreement is a complex agreement where all sub-agreements must be satisfied for the overall agreement to hold.

heading: YearlyRecurringEvent
text: A YearlyRecurringEvent recurs regularly on the same day of the same month, as specified by the hasMonth and dayOfMonth properties. As with MonthlyRecurringEvent, there may be ambiguity regarding the semantics of a yearly recurring event, however this formalization captures only the notion of an event that recurs on the same day of the same month (e.g. a birthday).

heading: Length
text: Length is the amount of space between two geographical points along a curve. It is a base quantity in the International System of Units and other systems of units. Length is speed times time. The metre, a base unit of length in the International System of Units, is defined in terms of speed of light during a certain time interval.

heading: NonTerminalState
text: A NonTerminalState has child States (a.k.a., sub-states) that are either conjuctive or disjunctive. Each child may be a TerminalState or NonTerminalState; eventually a TerminalState is reached. A State cannot be both non-terminal and terminal.

heading: TimeUnit
text: A specified system of units for time.

heading: State
text:
A State describes some situation in the world which may or may not be satisfied (true) at a given point in time. A State refers to a class of manifestations. It may be a precondition or effect of some Activity, or more generally it may be enabled or be caused by some Activity. If a state is complex, it may refer to some combination of classes of manifestations.
        Example: A shopping activity, Activity-Shop, can require both the VehicleW30LGas state, but also some state wherein the mall is

heading: Duration
text: Duration is the amount of time that elapses between two instants or events.

heading: SpatialLocThing
text: Added for organizational purposes, to identify all classes defined in the Location pattern of the CDM ontology.

heading: DisjunctiveState
text: A type of NonTerminalState that is defined by the disjunction of its child States. A State cannot be both conjunctive and disjunctive.

heading: Geo_Position_unit
text: Reference systems to indicate geographic position.

heading: ManifestationState
text: A specialization of TerminalState, the ManifestationState specifies a Manifestation class that an individual must satisfy in order for the ManifestationState to be true.

heading: MonthlyRecurringEvent
text: A MonthlyRecurringEvent recurs regularly on the same day of each month, as specified by the dayOfMonth data property. Note that there is often ambiguity regarding the semantics of a monthly recurring event: in this formalization, a MonthlyRecurringEvent is any event that recurs regularly on the same day of each month; other interpretations sometimes consider events that recur on the same day of week, or first or last day, in which case the day of month will vary.

heading: NonDivisibleResource
text: A Resource that can only be used for a single Activity at once - even if it isn't fully utilized.

heading: ActivityStatus
text: The status of the Activity.

heading: Manifestation
text: Manifestations may be interpreted as “snapshots” of an object at some point in time. This enables the representation of changing attributes of an object, without losing information about its past values/relationships. The properties of the class must then be identified as properties that are (and aren't) subject to change, in order to distinguish between the static and dynamic aspects of a particular entity.

heading: DivisibleResource
text: A Resource that can be divided for use or consumption between multiple Activities.

heading: Resource
text:
A Resource is a generic representation of some Thing that can be "used" in an Activity. 
        A Resource may have some Location, amount or availability, according to the definition of the Manifestation or TimeVaryingEntity. 
        A Resource must be classified as some Resource Type.
        A Resource may participate in some Activity Occurrence. In other words, it links to the Activity that the Resource is being used/consumed/produced/released at the time of the manifestation.
        A spe

heading: CityUnitsThing
text: Added for organizational purposes to group the classes defined in the City Units portion of the CDM.

heading: Speed
text: Speed is the time rate of motion measured by the distance moved over in unit time.

heading: Organization
text:
A collection of people organized together into a community or other social, commercial or political structure. The group has some common purpose or reason for existence which goes beyond the set of people belonging to it. An organization may itself be able to act as an agent.
        In addition to the standard org:Organization pattern, this ontology defines an cdm1:Organization to be a subclass of an cdm1:Agent.

heading: Location
text: A location.

heading: ComplexAgreement
text: A complex agreement is an agreement that is composed of two or more atomic agreements. It is a subclass of Agreement and specifies the “essence” of a complex agreement.  In particular it identifies how atomic agreements are composed to form a complex agreement.

heading: DisjunctiveAgreement
text: A disjunctive agreement is a complex agreement where at least one sub-agreement must be satisfied for the overall agreement to hold.

heading: RecurringEventThing
text: Class added for organizational purposes only; to group all classes defined in the recurring-event ontology.

heading: Area
text: Area expresses the two-dimensional size of a defined part of a surface, typically a region bounded by a closed curve. It is a derived quantity in the International System of Units. Area is length squared.

heading: ConsumeState
text: Identifies a Resource and Quantity it consumes. The Quantity is removed from the Resource.

heading: ProduceState
text: Identifies a Resource and Quantity it produces.

heading: AgreementThing
text: Added for organizational purposes, to identify all classes defined in the Agreement pattern.

heading: WeeklyRecurringEvent
text: A WeeklyRecurringEvent recurs on the regularly on the same day of the week, as specified by the schema:dayOfWeek property.

heading: ConjunctiveState
text: A type of NonTerminalState that is defined by the conjunction of its child States.

heading: Mass
text: Mass is the amount of matter of a phenomenon. It is a base quantity in the International System of Units. Mass is force divided by acceleration.

heading: MonetaryValue
text: A Monetary Value has a numerical value that is relative to a particular date (year). A Monetary Value is measured with some currency.

heading: UseState
text: Identifies a Resource and Quantity it uses (without consuming).

heading: Agent
text: An Agent affects, is affected by, or performs some Activity(s). An Agent may be a Person or Organization, but not Software or a Mechanical Device (at this time). An Agent can be a member of an organization and hold zero or more posts in an organization.

heading: Activity
text:
An Activity describes something that occurs in the domain.
An Activity may be further defined by (decomposed into) Subactivities.
An Activity may have precondition and/or effect State.
An Activity may be enabled by or cause some State. An enabling of causing state is a generalization of a precondition/effect; an Activity is enabled by or causes some State if it has a subactivity with a precondition or effect (respectively) of that State. 
In other words, the state may not be required directly be

heading: DailyRecurringEvent
text: A DailyRecurringEvent is a RecurringEvent that occurs every day. It has a maximum of one associated time, the start time. Typically, a daily recurring event will occur at the same time every day, but it is also possible that no commitment is made to a recurring start time for the event, in which case no start time is specified. A DailyEvent does not necessarily have the same endtime or duration, therefore these are not specified.

heading: FirstManifestation
text: The first recorded manifestation of an individual. No prior manifestations (in time) exist for the individual. It is a subclassOf Manifestation.

heading: Agreement
text: An agreement exists between two or more agents. It is established at some point in time and it may be considered valid only in some Location and/or for some interval in time. An agreement may be defined at varying levels of detail, this is supported with the introduction of the ComplexAgreement and AtomicAgreement class. Finally, agreements involve some specification of rights or commitments of the involved parties. This is represented as a relationship between the involved Agent and a particula

heading: ReleaseState
text: Identifies a Resource and Quantity it releases (after using).

heading: Volume
text: Volume is a measure of how much three-dimensional space any phenomenon occupies.

heading: Ratio
text: A ratio is a comparison of two quantities, often expressed as a fraction or in the form a:b.

heading: SpeedUnit
text: A specified system of units for speed.

heading: AgentThing
text: Added for organizational purposes, to identify all classes defined in the Agent pattern.

heading: AtomicAgreement
text: An atomic agreement is a simple agreement that cannot be further decomposed into sub-agreements. It is a subclass of Agreement and specifies the “essence” of an agreement.  In particular it identifies how agents participating in the agreement are involved: whether they have a claim, duty, no-claim or privilege with respect to some activity that is committed to in the agreement.

heading: TerminalState
text: A terminal state type has no substates (cannot be decomposed). It corresponds to a particular class of manifestations. A terminal state is achieved at some time if and only if there exists a manifestation within its defined classification, that exists at that time.

heading: VolumeUnit
text: A specified system of units for volume.

heading: ActivityThing
text: Added for organizational purposes, to identify all classes defined in the Activity pattern.

heading: ResourceThing
text: Added for organizational purposes, to identify all classes defined in the Resource ontology.

heading: ValueOfMoney
text: An amount of money that is defined relative to a particular year.

heading: RecurringEvent
text: Recurring events are defined based on the regular interval at which they occur; this is captures using some combination of the hasTime, dayOfWeek, hasMonth, and dayOfMonth properties.  Using these properties, ontology defines the following specializations of the RecurringEvent class. Other subclasses may be defined similarly, as required.

heading: OrganizationThing
text: A class representing an organization in the context of the CDM ontology.

heading: AreaUnit
text: Unit of area in the specified system of units.

heading: ChangeThing
text: Added for organizational purposes to group the classes defined in the Change portion of the CDM.

heading: LengthUnit
text: A specified system of units for distance.

heading: MereologyObjectProperty
text: Added for organizational purposes, to identify all mereology object properties defined in the City Data Model.

heading: aggregateOf
text: One object within a collection.

heading: aggregationOf
text: A collection of objects.

heading: componentOf
text: Specifies a part-whole relationship between objects where the component is a part that is defined based on actual boundaries. The parts are often also defined according to distinct functions. For example, a headlight is a componentOf a car.

heading: hasComponent
text: Specifies a part-whole relationship between objects where the component is a part that is defined based on actual boundaries. The parts are often also defined according to distinct functions. For example, a car hasComponent headlight.

heading: hasProperPart
text: Specifies a part-whole relationship between objects where an object cannot be part of itself.

heading: immediateComponentOf
text: Specifies a componentOf relationship where the if x immediateComponentOf y, then there does not exist a z where x immediateComponentOf z immediateComponentOf y.

heading: partOf
text: Specifies a part-whole relationship between objects

heading: properPartOf
text: Specifies a part-whole relationship between objects where an object cannot be part of itself

heading: CdmObjectProperty
text: Added for organizational purposes, to identify all object properties defined in the City Data Model.

heading: FoundationalCdmObjectProperty
text: Added for organizational purposes, to identify all foundational object properties defined in the City Data Model.

heading: ChangeObjectProperty
text: Added for organizational purposes, to identify all change object properties defined in the City Data Model.

heading: existsAt
text: Identifies the TemporalEntity that reflects the time Instant or Interval during which a Thing is valid.

heading: hasFirstManifestation
text: Identifies the First Manifestation that is related to a particular Manifestation (i.e., for some snapshot of an object, it identifies the first such snapshot of the object).

heading: hasLatestManifestation
text: Identifies the most recent Manifestation that is related to a particular FirstManifestation (i.e., for some snapshot of an object, it identifies the most recent snapshot of the object).

heading: hasManifestation
text: identifies a Manifestation that captures the instantiation of variant attributes of an object at some time (i.e., a temporal snapshot of some individual). The individual is related to all manifestations in a series via the hasManifestation property.

heading: hasNextManifestation
text: Identifies the immediate successor Manifestation (i.e. the subsequent snapshot of the object).

heading: hasPreviousManifestation
text: Identifies the immediate prior Manifestation (i.e. the prior snapshot of the object).

heading: precedesManifestation
text: Identifies all subsequent Manifestations that follow a FirstManifestation.

heading: CityUnitObjectProperty
text: Added for organizational purposes, to identify all city unit object properties defined in the City Data Model.

heading: AgentObjectProperty
text: Added for organizational purposes, to identify all agent object properties defined in the City Data Model.

heading: performs
text: Identifies activities that the Agent performs.

heading: RecurringEventObjectProperty
text: Added for organizational purposes, to identify all recurring event object properties defined in the City Data Model.

heading: beginsRecurringState
text: Defines a State that is required to be true in order to initiate the RecurringEvent.

heading: beginsRecurringTime
text: Specifies the start time of the RecurringEvent’s activity with a time:TemporalEntity from the Time pattern.

heading: endsRecurringState
text: Defines a State that is required to be true in order terminate the RecurringEvent.

heading: endsRecurringTime
text: Specifies the end time of the RecurringEvent’s activity with a time:TemporalEntity from the Time pattern

heading: hasDayOfWeek
text: Specifies the day of the week on which a Weekly RecurringEvent occurs with the time:DayOfWeek class from the Time pattern.

heading: hasOccurrence
text: Identifies the Activities that take place at the time and location.

heading: hasSubRecurringEvent
text: identifies the sub-recurring events that comprise the RecurringEvent. As with an Activity, a RecurringEvent may be decomposed/decomposed into simpler/more complex RecurringEvents to support varying levels of granularity.

heading: onDateTimeDescription
text: Specifies the date-time description (as defined in the Time pattern) on which a recurring event recurs. For example, the day of the month on which a MonthlyRecurringEvent recurs, or the day and month on which a YearlyRecurringEvent recurs.

heading: recursAddition
text: Defines a condition when the RecurringEvent should be added to. This can specify a time, day of the week, or specific dates.

heading: recursExcept
text: Defines the exceptions for the RecurringEvent, i.e. conditions when it does not occur. This can specify a time, day of the week, or specific dates.

heading: AgreementObjectProperty
text: Added for organizational purposes, to identify all agreement object properties defined in the City Data Model.

heading: commitsToActivity
text: A commitment to some activity

heading: establishedOn
text: Specifies the Instant of time at which the Agreement was created.

heading: hasClaim
text: Indicates that an Agent is the beneficiary of an Activity fulfilled by another Agent in the Agreement (i.e., the Agent with the duty to fulfil the Activity), e.g. payment of wages, provision of services.

heading: hasDuty
text: Indicates that an Agent is required to fulfil the described Activity.

heading: hasNoClaim
text: Indicates that an Agent has no claim on (i.e. has no right to the described Activity).

heading: hasPrivilege
text: Indicates that an Agent may but is not required to fulfil the described Activity.

heading: hasSubAgreement
text: Identifies two or more Agreements that comprise the Agreement.

heading: involvesAgent
text: Identifies the Agents that are party to the Agreement.

heading: validFor
text: Specifies the time Interval during which the Thing is valid or in force.

heading: validIn
text: Identifies the location where the Thing is valid.

heading: ResourceObjectProperty
text: Added for organizational purposes, to identify all resource object properties defined in the City Data Model.

heading: capacityInUse
text: Identifies the portion of capacity in use, by some Activity(s).

heading: forResource
text: Specifies the Resource that is allocated.

heading: forState
text: Specifies the State to which the Resource is allocated.

heading: hasAllocation
text: Specifies a Planned Allocation for the Resource. This indicates a State and time Interval to which the Resource has been allocated.

heading: hasAvailableCapacity
text: identifies the Quantity that specifies how much of the Resource is available for use.

heading: hasCapacity
text: Identifies the Quantity that specifies how much of the Resource exists, e.g. its volume, if it is liquid; how much it can hold, if it is an container.

heading: hasOwner
text: A Resource may have some owner. The owner of a resource is subject to change over time.

heading: hasQuantity
text: Specifies the Quantity of the Resource that participates in the State, if applicable.

heading: hasResource
text: Specifies a Resource that participates in the State. This identifies the condition of the object that plays a resource role (i.e. is used, consumed, etc.) when the state’s status is active.

heading: participatesIn
text: Identifies the Activity that the Resource is being used, consumed, produced, or released by.

heading: resourceOf
text: identifies what State the agent may be a resource of. This is specified using the Resource pattern, where the Agent would be considered to be a resource from the perspective of an Activity that uses it in some way. For example, a construction activity may use some persons(s) and those persons would be considered a Resource for that Activity.

heading: LocationObjectProperty
text: Added for organizational purposes, to identify all location object properties defined in the City Data Model.

heading: associatedLocation
text: Captures the association of a non-spatial object with a Location. For example, an activity is not a spatial object but may be associated with some location in which it (mostly) takes place.

heading: hasLocation
text: Specifies the Location of an object.

heading: ActivityObjectProperty
text: Added for organizational purposes, to identify all activity object properties defined in the City Data Model.

heading: achievedAt
text: Identifies the time Interval or Instant during which the State was satisfied.

heading: beginOf
text: Identifies the time Instant at which the Activity occurs.

heading: causedByActivity
text: Identifies an Activity that in some (indirect) way caused the State to be realized. The causedByActivity property is the inverse of the causes property and is a generalization (super-property) of the effectOf property.

heading: causesState
text: identifies a State that in some (indirect) way was caused by the occurrence of the Activity. An Activity is caused by a State if the State is an effect of the Activity or if the State is an effect of some subactivity of the Activity. The causes property is a generalization (super-property) of the hasEffect property.

heading: effectOf
text: Identifies an Activity that the State was realized by.

heading: enabledByState
text: Identifies a State that in some (indirect) way enabled the Activity to occur. An Activity is enabled by a State if the State is a precondition for the Activity or if the State is a precondition of some subactivity of the Activity. The enabledByState property is a generalization (super-property) of the hasPrecondition property.

heading: enablesActivity
text: Identifies an Activity that in some (indirect) way was enabled by the State. The enables property is the inverse of the enabledByState property and is a generalization (super-property) of the preconditionOf property.

heading: endOf
text: Identifies the time Instant at which the Activity ends.

heading: hasDecomp
text: Identifies two or more States into which the State may be immediately decomposed, i.e. its direct children.

heading: hasEffect
text: Identifies a State that is realized once the Activity has occurred.

heading: hasPrecondition
text: Identifies a State that must be realized in order for the Activity to occur.

heading: hasStatus
text: Identifies the status of the parent Thing at some point or interval in time.

heading: hasSubactivity
text: Identifies a more granular Activity into which the Activity may be decomposed.

heading: hasSubstate
text: Identifies a State into which the complex state decomposes, at any level (i.e. its child state, grandchild state, etc.). The hasSubState property is transitive and a super-property of the hasDecomp property.

heading: occursAt
text: Identifies a time Interval over which the Activity occurs.

heading: occursBefore
text: Identifies an Activity that the Activity occurred before.

heading: occursDirectlyBefore
text: Identifies an Activity that occurred immediately prior to the Activity. The occursDirectlyBefore property is a sub-property of the occursBefore property.

heading: preconditionOf
text: Identifies an Activity that requires the State to be realized in order to occur.

heading: satisfiedBy
text: Specifies the Manifestation that is to be satisfied (i.e., realized).

heading: scheduledFor
text: Identifies a time Interval during which a Thing occurs.

heading: CdmDataProperty
text: Added for organizational purposes, to identify all data properties defined in the City Data Model.

heading: FoundationalCdmDataProperty
text: Added for organizational purposes, to identify data properties defined in the Foundation-level CDM ontology.

heading: CityUnitsDataProperty
text: Added for organizational purposes, to identify data properties defined in the City Units Pattern of the CDM ontology.

heading: hasRelativeYear
text: The base year used to define monetary values for a currency.

heading: RecurringEventDataProperty
text: Added for organizational purposes, to identify object properties defined in the Recurring Event Properties Pattern of the CDM ontology.

heading: endTime
text: Specifies the end time of the domain object's activity.

heading: startTime
text: Specifies the start time of the RecurringEvent's activity using http://www.w3.org/2001/XMLSchema#time format.

heading: GeneralDataProperty
text: Added for organizational purposes, to identify object properties defined in the General Properties Pattern of the CDM ontology.

heading: hasDescription
text: Descriptive information about an object.

heading: hasIdentifier
text: Unique identifier for an object.

heading: hasName
text: Name of an object.

heading: LocationDataProperty
text: Created for organizational purposes, to identify location data properties.

heading: isInvariant
text: Identifies whether a property is to be interpreted as invariant (i.e., its value is not subject to change over time).

heading: mainModule
text: Identifies whether an ontology file is the main ontology file within a directory.
